description_long: This cowshed is a long, rectangular agricultural building with a dark, gently sloped gable roof extending the full length of the structure. The walls are constructed from what appears to be concrete or masonry, punctuated by a series of evenly spaced rectangular windows along both long sides, providing natural light and ventilation. The ends of the building feature two large, square doorways, likely for livestock entry and exit, with no visible ornamentation. The roof overhangs slightly, offering some protection from the elements at the sides. The overall appearance is utilitarian, prioritizing function over decorative elements, and the muted color palette suggests durability and ease of maintenance. The structure is typical of rural or peri-urban agricultural zones but could be found on the outskirts of urban environments where livestock facilities are permitted.
description: A long, rectangular cowshed with a dark gable roof, concrete walls, and rows of rectangular windows along both sides. Large square doorways are present at each end, emphasizing a functional, utilitarian design.
description_view_0: End view showing a dark gable roof and two large square doorways at the front, with minimal window visibility.
description_view_1: Side view displaying the full length of the cowshed, featuring a row of evenly spaced rectangular windows beneath the overhanging roof.
description_view_2: Opposite end view, nearly identical to view 0, with the gable roof and two large square doorways visible.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, mirroring view 1, with a continuous row of rectangular windows and the long, dark roof.
attribute_door_style: double square hinged, likely wood or metal, utilitarian
attribute_window_presence: multiple rectangular windows along both long sides, evenly spaced
